request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: SCOTUS rules Trump can fire FTC commissioners?
Kind: active
Timeline (5 events):
- 2026-05-11 [background] FTC Chairman Ferguson Advises Companies to Comply with the Take It Down Act — FTC chair advising compliance shows agency activity but gives no direct evidence that SCOTUS has ruled the President can remove FTC commissioners.
- 2026-05-01 [background] FTC to Host Workshop on “Litigate the Fix” Merger Remedy Proposals — FTC workshop on merger remedies is routine agency activity unrelated to the Supreme Court's removal‑power case; no material effect on YES odds.
- 2026-04-08 [background] Philip Serpe, In the Matter of (timeline item) - April 8, 2026 — An internal FTC timeline/item (Philip Serpe) with no evident link to the Supreme Court's decision; does not alter YES probability.
- 2026-03-12 [background] Rule Concerning the Use of Prenotification Negative Option Plans — FTC issuing a rule shows the agency is operating but provides no meaningful evidence on whether SCOTUS will permit presidential removal of commissioners — neutral.
- 2026-03-12 [background] Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Rental Housing Fee Practices — FTC rule on rental-housing fees is routine agency action and doesn't materially inform whether SCOTUS will allow at‑will removal of FTC commissioners.

response.text: The timeline shows routine FTC activity—new rules on negative‑option plans and rental‑fee practices, a merger‑remedy workshop, and internal updates—plus an advisory from the chair, indicating agency normalcy. None of this addresses presidential removal authority, so the market still awaits any Supreme Court movement or ruling on whether the President can fire FTC commissioners.
